subject: UX & Research
title: Why Search Fails
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Why Search Fails.

hook.text: Users type 'how to cancel' into site search. Results show cancellation policy articles. Not the cancel button.

buildup.text: Most site search returns content pages, not actions. Users searching for tasks get informational results instead.

discovery.text: Baymard found 70% of e-commerce search engines fail at synonyms. 'Sofa' returns nothing because the site says 'couch.'

twist.text: Many sites don't even track failed searches. They don't know what users want but can't find.

climax.text: Track zero-result searches. They're a direct signal of user need and vocabulary mismatch.

punchline.text: Search fails silently. Track zero-result queries to find the gaps.

quiz.question: What is the most common reason site search fails users?
options: Poor synonym handling — the site uses different words than users type
Search bars are too small to notice
Users don't know how to use search engines
